items.t: — the worst week in six arrives mid-war; December hike odds hit 73% and the haven bid loses to the rate bet
— the first F.P. Journe Résonance ever delivered heads to Phillips Geneva in November, est. CHF 0.5–1M after a $23M year for the model
— Audemars Piguet's UK sales jump 29% while operating profit slips 3%; the Manchester AP House lost £738,000 finding its feet
— nearly two in five Rolexes submitted to the marketplace fail authentication; Omega runs at 13%, Tudor 6%
— the jewelry show returns to Southampton July 23–26 with Italian and studio-artist pavilions; VIP preview $200
— Brandee Dallow, ex-De Beers and Rio Tinto, takes over as CEO of the colored-stone association's 700 members in 47 countries
— Guess ships a $250, 200-piece watch with an AI-drawn print; WatchPro says it could pass for a Richard Mille across a room
— five decades on 47th Street become a memoir: 'Unveiling Rarities' recounts 1976 to now, including the 1999 robbery
— the gold-silver ratio stretches to 72 as rate anxiety hits the white metal hardest; down nearly 15% on the month
— Logan calls publicly for a hike, Jefferson signals openness; the meeting is now the metals trade's main event
